---
title: "Readings"
---

Everything you need to prepare for each session, with direct links. Only three kinds of items are **not** linked directly from this page, because they are licensed or graded:

- **Textbook chapters** (Fabozzi; Ramsinghani for Session 12) — buy the book or read it through Yale Library ([details below](#textbook)).
- **The HBS FeedBurner case** — licensed; get it through the coursepack link on [Canvas](https://yale.instructure.com/courses/122169).
- **The Solstice Robotics case packet** — a graded case, released on [Canvas](https://yale.instructure.com/courses/122169) on September 21.

Everything else — podcasts, articles, guides, workshop materials — is linked here and on the [Schedule](schedule.qmd).

## The textbook {#textbook}

**Frank J. Fabozzi, *Entrepreneurial Finance and Accounting for High-Tech Companies* (MIT Press, 2016)** — the course textbook; chapter and page assignments are listed per session below.

- [MIT Press page](https://mitpress.mit.edu/9780262034982/entrepreneurial-finance-and-accounting-for-high-tech-companies/) (ISBN 978-0-262-03498-2)
- [Yale Library catalog](https://search.library.yale.edu/catalog?q=Entrepreneurial+Finance+and+Accounting+for+High-Tech+Companies+Fabozzi&search_field=all_fields) — check for the e-book before buying

**Mahendra Ramsinghani, *The Business of Venture Capital* (Wiley)** — Chapter 25 only, for Session 12 (exits).

- [Yale Library catalog](https://search.library.yale.edu/catalog?q=The+Business+of+Venture+Capital+Ramsinghani&search_field=all_fields) — the Wiley e-book is the easiest route for a single chapter

## Readings by session

```{python}
#| echo: false
#| output: asis
import yaml
from datetime import datetime

with open("schedule.yml") as f:
    sessions = yaml.safe_load(f)

for entry in sorted(
    (e for e in sessions if e.get("session") is not None),
    key=lambda e: str(e["date"]),
):
    sess = entry["session"]
    d = datetime.strptime(str(entry["date"]), "%Y-%m-%d").strftime("%a %b %-d")
    topic = entry.get("topic", "")
    prep = entry.get("prep") or []
    print(f"### Session {sess} — {topic}")
    print(f"*{d}*")
    print()
    if not prep:
        print("No advance reading — just show up.")
    for item in prep:
        text = item.get("text", "")
        url = item.get("url")
        note = item.get("note")
        line = f"[{text}]({url})" if url else f"{text}"
        if note:
            line += f" — *{note}*"
        print(f"- {line}")
    print()
```

## Optional background readings

Not required; for students who want to go deeper.

- Fabozzi Chs. 9–11 (the financial statements) and Ch. 26 (IPOs) — [textbook](#textbook)
- Doidge, Karolyi, and Stulz (2017), [*The U.S. Listing Gap*](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jfineco.2016.11.002), Journal of Financial Economics
- Ball and Shivakumar (2008), [*Earnings Quality at Initial Public Offerings*](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jacceco.2007.12.001), Journal of Accounting and Economics
- Hellmann and Wasserman (2017), [*The First Deal: The Division of Founder Equity in New Ventures*](https://doi.org/10.1287/mnsc.2016.2474), Management Science
- Bengtsson and Hand (2009), [*CEO Compensation in Venture-Backed Firms*](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=Bengtsson+Hand+CEO+compensation+venture-backed+firms)

Journal links go through the publisher (DOI) — use the [Yale Library proxy](https://library.yale.edu/) or campus network for full text.

---

See also: [Schedule](schedule.qmd) · [Materials](materials.qmd) (slides and handouts) · [Cases](cases.qmd) · [Problem Sets](assignments.qmd)
